description: A green-energy mining conglomerate is evaluating a new lithium extraction facility on the Tibetan Plateau (Zabuye Salt Lake) to complement operations in the Andes (Salar de Atacama). Lithium is extracted via massive surface evaporation ponds, which require high temperatures and minimal precipitation. Analyze January surface air temperature climatology to determine if winter lithium brine evaporation is feasible on the Tibetan Plateau, comparing it against the Andean baseline. Use Panoply and the data in ~/PanoplyData/ to complete the analysis requested in the project brief (~/Desktop/lithium_prospecting_brief.txt). Convert temperatures from Kelvin to Celsius and export all required outputs to ~/Documents/LithiumProspecting/.
